# quent

> Transparent sync/async bridge for Python. Write pipeline code once -- quent detects awaitables at runtime and handles them automatically. Pure Python, zero dependencies, PEP 561 typed.

quent provides a fluent `Chain` class for building sequential pipelines. The same chain definition works for both sync and async callables with no code duplication. Execution starts synchronously; if any step returns an awaitable, quent seamlessly transitions to an async path. `.run()` returns either a plain value or a coroutine -- the caller decides whether to `await`.

- Package: quent
- Python: >=3.10
- License: MIT
- Dependencies: None (pure Python on 3.11+; typing_extensions on 3.10)
- Typing: PEP 561 (py.typed + inline annotations)
- Install: `pip install quent`
- GitHub: https://github.com/drukmano/quent
- Docs: https://quent.readthedocs.io
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/quent/

## Documentation

- [Full Documentation](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/drukmano/quent)
- [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/quent/)
- [Changelog](https://github.com/drukmano/quent/releases)

## Quick Start

```python
from quent import Chain

# Basic pipeline
result = Chain([1, 2, 3]).foreach(lambda x: x * 2).then(sum).run()
# 12

# Same chain works with async callables -- no changes needed
result = await Chain(url).then(fetch).then(process).run()

# Side effects with .do() -- result discarded, value passes through
result = Chain(data).do(log).then(transform).do(save).run()

# Error handling -- handler receives ChainExcInfo(exc, root_value)
result = Chain(url).then(fetch).except_(lambda ei: default_value).run()

# Reuse via clone
base = Chain().then(validate).then(transform)
branch_a = base.clone().then(save_to_db)
branch_b = base.clone().then(save_to_file)
```

## API Overview

- [Chain(v=Null, *args, **kwargs)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#constructor) -- Constructor with optional root value.
- [.then(v, *args, **kwargs)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#then) -- Append step; result replaces current value.
- [.do(fn, *args, **kwargs)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#do) -- Side-effect step; result discarded.
- [.foreach(fn, *, concurrency=None, executor=None)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#map) -- Apply fn to each element, collect results.
- [.foreach_do(fn, *, concurrency=None, executor=None)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#foreach_do) -- Side-effect per element, keep originals.
- [.gather(*fns, concurrency=-1, executor=None)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#gather) -- Run multiple functions concurrently on current value.
- [.with_(fn, *args, **kwargs)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#with_) -- Enter current value as context manager, call fn.
- [.with_do(fn, *args, **kwargs)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#with_do) -- Context manager side-effect variant.
- [.if_(predicate=None, *args, **kwargs)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#if_) -- Begin conditional; must be followed by .then() or .do().
- [.else_(v, *args, **kwargs)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#else_) -- Else branch for preceding if_().
- [.else_do(fn, *args, **kwargs)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#else_do) -- Side-effect else branch.
- [.except_(fn, *args, exceptions=None, reraise=False, **kwargs)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#except_) -- Exception handler (one per chain).
- [.finally_(fn, *args, **kwargs)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#finally_) -- Cleanup handler (one per chain, always runs).
- [.name(label)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#name) -- Assign a label for traceback identification.
- [.run(v=Null, *args, **kwargs)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#run) -- Execute chain.
- [.decorator()](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#decorator) -- Wrap chain as function decorator.
- [.iterate(fn=None)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#iterate) -- Sync/async iterator over chain output.
- [.iterate_do(fn=None)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#iterate_do) -- Iterator with side-effect fn.
- [.clone()](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#clone) -- Independent copy of chain.
- [Chain.return_(v=Null, *args, **kwargs)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#return_) -- Early return from chain.
- [Chain.break_(v=Null, *args, **kwargs)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#break_) -- Break from iteration; value appended to partial results.
- [Chain.on_step](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#on_step) -- Class-level step instrumentation callback.
- [ChainExcInfo](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#chainexcinfo) -- NamedTuple (exc, root_value) passed to except handlers.
- [Null](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#null-sentinel) -- Sentinel for "no value provided".
- [QuentException](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#quentexception) -- Public exception type.

## Key Concepts

- [Calling Conventions](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/guide/chains/) -- Two rules: explicit args override current value; no args passes current value as first argument.
- [Sync/Async Bridging](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/guide/async/) -- Execution starts sync; transitions to async on first awaitable; caller decides whether to await.
- [Enhanced Tracebacks](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/) -- Chain visualization injected into exceptions with `<----` marker pointing to failing step.

- [Source Code](https://github.com/drukmano/quent/tree/master/quent)
